summary: This brief post notes two items: a new CityDesk for Geeks page on the Fog Creek website, and a tribute to Dave Thomas, founder of Wendy's, who passed away. Spolsky reflects on how Thomas's personal appearance in hundreds of commercials gave Wendy's a human, approachable identity. He draws a broader lesson about business: customers prefer buying from people they can see and trust rather than faceless corporations.
key_points: Dave Thomas personified his brand by appearing in hundreds of Wendy's commercials, creating trust and warmth
Customers inherently prefer buying from identifiable people rather than anonymous corporations
A founder's personal involvement and visibility can be a powerful competitive advantage
money_quotes: He taught us that nobody, given the choice, prefers to buy from a faceless megacorporation.
key_insight: People prefer to buy from visible, accountable individuals rather than faceless corporations, and a founder's personal presence can be a brand's greatest asset.
